agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Leave management module within an HR software system. Governs how employees accrue, request, consume, and carry over paid leave. Enforces accrual-based balance integrity, advance-notice policies, manager approval workflows, and year-end carryover caps.","bounded_context":"Leave Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Leave Balance","definition":"The current number of accrued but unused leave days available to an employee. Must never go negative."},{"term":"Accrual","definition":"The process by which leave days are earned. Employees accrue 1.5 days per month worked."},{"term":"Leave Request","definition":"A formal submission by an employee to use accrued leave days for a specified date range, subject to advance-notice rules and manager approval."},{"term":"Medical Leave","definition":"A special category of leave that may be submitted retroactively (within 5 calendar days of the absence) provided a doctor's note is attached."},{"term":"Working Days","definition":"Business days excluding weekends and company-recognized holidays, used to calculate the advance-notice requirement."},{"term":"Advance Notice Period","definition":"The minimum 3 working days between submission of a non-medical leave request and its start date."},{"term":"Contract End Date","definition":"The date on which an employee's employment contract terminates. Leave requests must not span beyond this date."},{"term":"Carryover","definition":"The process at calendar year-end where unused leave days are transferred to the next year, capped at 10 days. Days above 10 are forfeited."},{"term":"Forfeiture","definition":"The permanent loss of unused leave days exceeding the 10-day carryover cap at calendar year-end."},{"term":"Approval","definition":"A manager's decision to grant a leave request, which then deducts the requested days from the employee's leave balance."},{"term":"Rejection","definition":"A manager's decision to deny a leave request. Rejected requests do not consume any leave balance."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"Employees accrue 1.5 days of leave per month worked.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Accrual rate is a policy parameter that HR leadership could adjust. Not a data-integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"Given an employee who has worked N complete months, their total accrued leave equals N × 1.5 days (before any deductions for taken leave)."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Leave balance must never go negative. Leave cannot be taken before it is accrued.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Negative balance would mean consuming a resource that does not exist — a fundamental state-integrity violation akin to overdrawing a non-overdraft account.","testable_assertion":"At no point in time shall an employee's leave balance be less than zero. Any operation that would result in a negative balance must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"Non-medical leave requests must be submitted at least 3 working days before the leave start date.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 3-working-day notice window is an operational policy that management could shorten or lengthen.","testable_assertion":"A non-medical leave request submitted fewer than 3 working days before its start date is rejected at submission time."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Medical leave can be submitted retroactively within 5 days of the absence, provided a doctor's note is attached.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 5-day window and documentation requirement are HR policy, not a system integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"A medical leave request submitted more than 5 days after the absence date is rejected. A medical leave request submitted within 5 days but without a doctor's note attachment is rejected."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Only a manager can approve or reject a leave request.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Authorization constraint — allowing non-managers to approve would violate the organizational authority model and is a security/integrity concern.","testable_assertion":"Leave request status can only transition to approved or rejected by a user with the manager role for the requesting employee."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Rejected leave does not consume leave balance.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Deducting balance for a rejected request would corrupt the balance — this is a state-integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"When a leave request is rejected, the employee's leave balance remains unchanged from its value before the request was submitted."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"Leave requests cannot span across (extend beyond) the employee's contract end date.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"An employee cannot be on leave when they are no longer employed — this is a real-world physical/legal constraint on system state.","testable_assertion":"A leave request whose date range includes any date on or after the employee's contract end date is rejected at submission time."},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"At calendar year-end, unused leave above 10 days is forfeited. A maximum of 10 days carries over to the next year.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 10-day carryover cap is a policy threshold that HR leadership could change.","testable_assertion":"On January 1, if an employee's leave balance exceeds 10 days, it is set to 10. If it is 10 or fewer, it remains unchanged."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"An employee has worked 4 complete months and taken no leave","when":"The system calculates their leave balance","then":"The balance equals 6.0 days (4 × 1.5)"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"An employee has a leave balance of 2 days","when":"They submit a non-medical leave request for 3 days","then":"The request is rejected with an insufficient-balance error and the balance remains 2 days"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"An employee has a leave balance of 5 days","when":"They submit a non-medical leave request for 3 days with a start date 2 working days from now","then":"The request is rejected with an insufficient-notice error and the balance remains 5 days"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"An employee has a leave balance of 5 days","when":"They submit a non-medical leave request for 3 days with a start date 3 working days from now","then":"The request is accepted into pending status awaiting manager approval"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"An employee was absent 4 days ago due to illness","when":"They submit a medical leave request with a valid doctor's note attached","then":"The request is accepted into pending status despite being retroactive"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"An employee was absent 6 days ago due to illness","when":"They submit a medical leave request with a doctor's note","then":"The request is rejected because it exceeds the 5-day retroactive submission window"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"An employee submits a medical leave request within 5 days of absence","when":"No doctor's note is attached","then":"The request is rejected for missing required documentation"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A leave request is in pending status","when":"A manager approves the request","then":"The request status changes to approved and the employee's leave balance is reduced by the number of requested days"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A leave request is in pending status","when":"A manager rejects the request","then":"The request status changes to rejected and the employee's leave balance remains unchanged"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"An employee's contract ends on March 15","when":"They submit a leave request for March 14–16","then":"The request is rejected because it spans beyond the contract end date"},{"id":"AC-11","given":"An employee has 14 unused leave days on December 31","when":"The year-end carryover process runs","then":"Their balance on January 1 is set to 10 days and 4 days are recorded as forfeited"},{"id":"AC-12","given":"An employee has 8 unused leave days on December 31","when":"The year-end carryover process runs","then":"Their balance on January 1 remains 8 days with no forfeiture"},{"id":"AC-13","given":"A non-manager user attempts to approve or reject a leave request","when":"The action is submitted","then":"The system denies the action with an authorization error"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Employee submits leave starting exactly 3 working days from today (boundary of advance notice)","expected_behavior":"Request is accepted — the rule is 'at least 3 working days' so exactly 3 is valid."},{"scenario":"Leave request ends exactly on the contract end date (last day of contract)","expected_behavior":"The employee is still employed on the contract end date minus one day. Clarify whether the contract end date is the last working day or the first non-working day. Conservatively, if the end date means 'last day employed,' leave on that date should be allowed; if it means 'first day not employed,' leave that day should be rejected."},{"scenario":"Medical leave submitted on exactly the 5th day after absence","expected_behavior":"Accepted — 'within 5 days' includes the 5th day (boundary inclusive)."},{"scenario":"Employee accrues leave mid-month and requests leave for the same month","expected_behavior":"Balance check uses the accrued balance at the time of request submission. If the accrual for the current month has not yet posted, those days are not available."},{"scenario":"Multiple pending leave requests that collectively exceed the balance","expected_behavior":"The system should reserve (hold) days for pending-approved requests, or at minimum validate at approval time that balance is still sufficient. Otherwise two overlapping approvals could overdraw the balance."},{"scenario":"Year-end carryover when employee has pending leave requests spanning the year boundary","expected_behavior":"Forfeiture calculation must account for pending requests — forfeiting days that are already committed to approved future leave would violate the non-negative balance invariant."},{"scenario":"Manager is also the employee submitting leave","expected_behavior":"A manager should not be able to approve their own leave request — a separation-of-duties concern. The spec does not address this explicitly."},{"scenario":"Leave request for a single day that is a public holiday or weekend","expected_behavior":"System should validate that requested leave days are actual working days. Taking leave on a non-working day would waste balance."},{"scenario":"Contract end date falls within the advance notice period","expected_behavior":"If an employee's contract ends in 2 working days, they cannot submit non-medical leave at all since the 3-day notice cannot be satisfied before contract end."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Correct accrual and carryover enforcement prevents liability inflation from over-accrued or improperly carried-over leave. Forfeiture rules cap the financial obligation the company carries on its books at year-end.","operational":"Advance notice requirements enable workforce planning. Manager approval gates prevent unauthorized absences. Automated balance enforcement eliminates manual reconciliation errors.","user":"Employees gain transparency into their available balance and clear rules for requesting time off. Retroactive medical leave with documentation provides necessary flexibility for genuine illness.","risk":"Primary risk is balance corruption — if the non-negative invariant is violated, the company either owes unearned leave or must retroactively deny time off. Secondary risk is year-end carryover miscalculation leading to financial misstatement of leave liabilities.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Employees","impact":"Direct users of the system. Accrual accuracy and balance transparency affect their ability to plan time off. Forfeiture rules affect year-end decisions."},{"group":"Managers","impact":"Responsible for approving/rejecting requests. Need visibility into team leave schedules to maintain adequate staffing."},{"group":"HR / People Operations","impact":"Owns leave policy configuration (accrual rates, carryover caps, notice periods). Needs audit trails for compliance and dispute resolution."},{"group":"Finance / Payroll","impact":"Leave balances represent a financial liability on the balance sheet. Accurate accrual and forfeiture directly affect financial reporting."},{"group":"Legal / Compliance","impact":"Medical leave documentation requirements must align with labor law. Retroactive submission windows and contract-end constraints have legal implications."}]},"confidence":{"level":82,"reasoning":"The specification is clear, internally consistent, and covers the core lifecycle of leave management. Rules are explicit and testable. Deducting points because several operational details are unspecified (see low-confidence areas) but none represent contradictions — they are reasonable gaps in a module-level spec.","high_confidence_areas":["Accrual rate and calculation","Non-negative balance invariant","Advance notice rules and medical leave exception","Carryover cap and forfeiture at year-end","Manager approval/rejection workflow","Contract end date boundary"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether 'contract end date' means last day employed or first day not employed — affects boundary validation","When exactly monthly accrual is posted (1st of month, last day, pro-rata for partial months)","Whether pending requests reserve balance or only deduct on approval — critical for concurrent request handling","Whether '5 days' for medical leave means calendar days or working days","Self-approval by managers — separation of duties not addressed","Whether leave days must fall on working days only","How partial-month accrual works for new hires or terminations mid-month"],"assumptions":["'5 days' for retroactive medical leave submission means 5 calendar days","Accrual of 1.5 days occurs once per calendar month on a fixed schedule","Leave balance is checked and deducted at approval time, not submission time","The 3-working-day notice period excludes the submission day and the leave start day","Contract end date is the last day the employee is employed (inclusive)"]},"escalations":[],"rejection_reasons":[]}

content.thinking: Clear spec, no contradictions or ambiguities. Let me analyze this leave management domain.
